title: Logical operators should not be confused with bitwise operators
description: Logical operators should not be confused with bitwise operatorsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhile working with bitwise operators&or|, it is easy to make a typo and write the equivalent logical operators&&or||. This rule raises an issue when the right operand of a logical expression&&or||is a constant of integral type, as the developer probably meant to use the corresponding bitwise operator&or|.Noncompliant code exampleint fun(int a) { return a || 4; // Noncompliant: did you mean to use bitwise operator '|'? }Compliant solutionint fun(int a) { return a | 4; }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-pre
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int result = value || 4; // Noncompliant: probably meant bitwise OR
if (flags && 0x80) // Noncompliant: probably meant bitwise AND
return status || MASK_BIT; // Noncompliant: logical OR with constant
bool check = (x && 0xFF); // Noncompliant: logical AND with bitmask
compliant: int result = value | 4; // Compliant: bitwise OR
if (flags & 0x80) // Compliant: bitwise AND for flag checking
return status | MASK_BIT; // Compliant: bitwise OR
bool check = (x & 0xFF) != 0; // Compliant: bitwise AND with proper comparison

checks.message: Logical operators should not be confused with bitwise operators - use & instead of && or | instead of || when working with integral constants
